quickSummary: Dow suffered worst week since April as oil hit $90 on Iran escalation and NFP showed -92K jobs with unemployment at 4.4%. Fear & Greed dropped to 27 (Fear). Insider buying accelerated — TTD $148M, KKR Co-CEOs $13.5M combined, NOW CEO $3M. Coughlin Cap: BABA mispriced at RSI 22. Crack The Market: Nebius as purest AI compute play. VC Corner: SaaS valuations reset to ~5x revenue.

keyThemes.title: NFP Catastrophe: -92K Jobs
keyThemes.description: US economy lost 92,000 jobs in February, unemployment rose to 4.4%. Worst week since April for the Dow. Mortgage rates hit 6%.

keyThemes.title: Alibaba Mispriced at RSI 22
keyThemes.description: Coughlin Cap argues BABA selloff is geopolitical dislocation, not business deterioration. Hang Seng at 2-month lows, KOSPI triggered trading halt.

keyThemes.title: Nebius: Purest AI Compute Play
keyThemes.description: Crack The Market's most comprehensive NBIS case: full-stack vertical integration, TCO advantage over hyperscalers, >3GW capacity expansion.

keyThemes.title: KKR Insider Cluster Buy
keyThemes.description: Both Co-CEOs (Bae, Nuttall) + director Barakett bought ~$13.5M combined. TTD CEO Green's $148M purchase remains the week's dominant signal.

keyThemes.title: The Great SaaS Reset
keyThemes.description: VC Corner: median public software now trades at ~5x revenue (down from 50-100x in 2021). Capital efficiency replaces growth-at-all-costs.

fearGreedIndex.label: Fear
fearGreedIndex.context: Down 7 points. NFP -92K jobs, unemployment 4.4%, oil at $90, Dow worst week since April. 1 week ago: Fear (38). 1 month ago: Neutral (46). Approaching Extreme Fear territory.

stockHighlights.context: Coughlin Cap: 'Mispriced' — RSI ~22, capitulation territory amid Asia-wide selloff
Crack The Market: Pure-play AI Neocloud, full-stack vertical integration, >3GW expansion
Sleep Well: Q4'25 -16% to ~$88 on Shopee margin guidance (0.55% FY27 vs 0.7% FY25)
CEO Jeff Green $148M purchase (+373% position) continues to reverberate
CEO Isaacman total purchases ~$15.7M across multiple tranches

high: NBIS — Purest AI compute deep dive

insiderBuysNote: Top 20 open-market purchases from SEC Form 4 filings (Feb 27 – Mar 6, 2026).
insiderBuysSignals: TTD CEO Jeff Green's $148M purchase remains the dominant signal. KKR cluster buy: both Co-CEOs + director totaling ~$13.5M. GeneDx (WGS) Corvex's Keith Meister added $13.5M. Shift4 CEO Isaacman total >$15.7M. New: Hycroft Mining (HYMC) Eric Sprott $4.8M, American Bitcoin (ABTC) Tinder co-founder Justin Mateen $1.85M (+248% position).

macroOverview.author: Darius Dale
macroOverview.title: Worst week since April — NFP miss compounds geopolitical shock

themes.title: Energy
themes.description: Oil at $90; Hormuz Strait disruption fears intensifying.

themes.title: Equities
themes.description: Dow worst week since April; S&P 6,740. AI earnings provide counterbalance.

themes.title: Insider Buying
themes.description: $350M+ in open-market purchases this week — historically bullish contrarian signal.

themes.title: Fed Policy
themes.description: Rate cuts pushed further out; energy-driven inflation risk.

themes.title: Bonds

posts.title: Alibaba is Mispriced
posts.summary: BABA at RSI ~22 in low $130s — full capitulation territory. Selloff driven by Asia-wide geopolitical dislocation (Hang Seng 2-month lows, KOSPI trading halt, billions in HK outflows), not Alibaba business deterioration.

posts.title: Nebius: The Purest Way to Play AI Compute
posts.summary: Most comprehensive NBIS analysis online. Pure-play AI Neocloud with full-stack vertical integration, TCO advantage over hyperscalers, >3GW capacity expansion without toxic debt. Compelling risk-reward with hyper-growth compressing multiples.

posts.title: Sea Limited (SE) Q4'25 — Shopee in Focus
posts.summary: Shares -16% to ~$88. Shopee margin guided 0.55% FY27 vs 0.7% FY25. Author bullish on decade-long thesis: ~400M buyers, ~20M sellers, ~$3.5B FCF. Margin sacrifice seen as temporary.

posts.title: TMTB Morning Wrap
posts.summary: Daily morning market wrap covering tech sector. Follows MS TMT Conference coverage and Broadcom earnings reaction.

posts.title: Claude Code and Codex bet on different harnesses
posts.summary: Stop comparing models, start comparing harnesses. Claude Code vs Codex represent different orchestration approaches. Includes 2 prompts to audit which harness your team is compounding.

posts.title: Grab the prompt kit to audit your AI platform lock-in
posts.summary: Practical toolkit for auditing AI platform lock-in. Switching costs compounding past the point of no return. Prompts to assess vendor dependency.

posts.title: Claude won 4 of 8 blind writing tests
posts.summary: Claude leads reasoning by 14.6 points, follows instructions 94% of the time. No single metric tells the full story — the real question is workflow fit.

posts.title: How a Taiwanese VC Broke Into the Commercial Space Industry
posts.summary: Profile of Liang-rong Chen in commercial space. Apple budget i17 at $799 ($100 cheaper). Memory prices soaring; Chinese brands preparing 15-20% price increases.

posts.title: The Great SaaS Reset: From 30x Euphoria to AI-Driven Repricing
posts.summary: Median public software at ~5x revenue (some near 4x), down from 50-100x in 2021. Capital efficiency and durable retention replace growth-at-all-costs. The whole SaaS industry is being repriced.

posts.title: The Quiet Shift Behind Modern Coaching Businesses
posts.summary: Expert-led businesses moving from people-based to system-based support. Real value in ideas and relationships; everything else should be systematized.

posts.title: War, Oil, and the Hidden Variable in the Outsourcing Market
posts.summary: Iran conflict could reshape outsourcing demand. Cost shocks from rising oil ripple through corporate spending, historically driving outsourcing adoption.
